description_long: This is a commercial passenger airplane with a sleek, elongated fuselage painted primarily in white with a dark blue tail and underbelly, accented by red and silver stripes. The aircraft features a low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and prominent winglets at the tips. Two jet engines are mounted at the rear of the fuselage, just below the T-tail, which consists of a horizontal stabilizer mounted atop a single vertical fin. The cockpit is located at the very front of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the flight crew. The landing gear is a tricycle type, with the main gear under the wings and a nose gear beneath the cockpit. The livery displays the Aeroflot branding, indicating it is modeled after a real-world airline.
description: A sleek, white commercial passenger airplane with a blue and red tail, rear-mounted engines, and a T-tail design. The aircraft features swept low wings with winglets and Aeroflot branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, swept low wings, rear-mounted engines, and the Aeroflot livery.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the T-tail, twin rear-mounted engines, and the full wingspan with visible winglets.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with clear visibility of the tail and engine placement.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the overall planform, wing sweep, winglets, and the arrangement of the engines and tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and upward-angled winglets at the tips.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted at the rear fuselage, below the tail.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer mounted at the top.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the fuselage.
